repo_notes.content: this index.html will be use and display in a ReTerminal E1002 epaper device.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to KmCal — what it is, what it does, how the pieces fit together, and links to child pages for deeper detail.

pages.title: Project Purpose and Architecture
pages.purpose: Explains the overall goal of KmCal (a Singapore-focused family calendar viewer hosted on GitHub Pages), the three data sources it aggregates, and how the static-site + GitHub Actions architecture works end-to-end.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for a new contributor: how to clone the repo, open the calendar locally, understand the file layout, and trigger the GitHub Actions workflows manually.

pages.title: Calendar Data Sources
pages.purpose: Overview of the three ICS data files that feed the viewer, their origins, formats, and update cadences. Links to child pages for each source.

pages.title: Singapore Public Holidays (basic.ics)
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for basic.ics — its iCalendar structure, the range of years covered (2021–2032), event classification (Public holiday vs. Observance), tentative lunar-based dates, compensatory day-off logic, and how it is auto-updated from Google Calendar.

pages.title: Family Calendar (calendar.ics)
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for calendar.ics — its Apple iCloud CalDAV origin, event types (timed, all-day, recurring via RRULE), VTIMEZONE definitions for Asia/Singapore, and how it is auto-synced hourly from iCloud.

pages.title: School Holidays (school-holidays.ics)
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for school-holidays.ics — its static structure, the four 2026 holiday blocks it defines, PRODID/UID conventions, CLASS:PUBLIC and TRANSP:TRANSPARENT semantics, and how to update it for future years.

pages.title: Web Calendar Viewer (index.html)
pages.purpose: Overview of the single-page calendar application — its rendering model, data pipeline, and visual design. Links to child pages covering the data pipeline and the UI/CSS layer.

pages.title: Data Acquisition and Parsing Pipeline
pages.purpose: Deep dive into how index.html fetches, parses, and aggregates the three ICS feeds: the ICS_URLS constant, synchronous XMLHttpRequest pattern, parseICS line-unfolding logic, parseICSDatetime format handling, __source tagging, buildEventsByDate multi-day expansion, and the eventsByDate map.

pages.title: Rendering Engine and UI
pages.purpose: Covers the calendar rendering lifecycle: getSingaporeToday timezone anchoring, the 42-cell 6-week grid generated by renderCalendar, CSS class assignment (day-today, day-weekend, day-future-month, other-month), color-coded event badges (event-basic, event-school-holidays, event-calendar), and the appendEvents injection pattern.

pages.title: Automation and CI/CD
pages.purpose: Overview of the two GitHub Actions workflows that keep the ICS data files current, their shared design pattern, and links to child pages for each workflow.

pages.title: Public Holiday Sync Workflow (update_PH_ics.yml)
pages.purpose: Full reference for the update_PH_ics.yml GitHub Actions workflow: schedule (every 12 hours), workflow_dispatch trigger, curl download from Google Calendar's public ICS endpoint, git diff --cached change-detection guard, contents:write permission, and commit/push logic.

pages.title: Family Calendar Sync Workflow (update-ics.yml)
pages.purpose: Full reference for the update-ics.yml GitHub Actions workflow: hourly cron schedule, workflow_dispatch trigger, curl download from the iCloud CalDAV published URL, change-detection guard, and commit/push logic. Includes notes on the iCloud public publishing URL format.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, iCalendar standard vocabulary, abbreviations, and domain concepts that an onboarding engineer needs to understand KmCal. Each entry includes file/line pointers.
